hypothesis: Incorporating multi-modal electron beam dose rate as a dynamic input channel during SAM-EM inference—rather than treating it as a fixed experimental parameter—will enable the model to adaptively recalibrate its segmentation confidence thresholds in real time, such that particles undergoing beam-induced motion artifacts are distinguished from those exhibiting intrinsic stochastic diffusion, thereby reducing false identity-switching events by a measurable and statistically significant margin compared to the current dose-agnostic fine-tuned model.
